hotspots.title: Vox’s ‘national priority’ surge in Spain
hotspots.blurb: Spain’s far-right Vox party has secured regional coalition deals with the conservative PP in Extremadura, Aragón, and Valencia, centering on the controversial 'prioridad nacional' policy. This principle prioritizes Spanish citizens in access to social housing, welfare, and public services, sparking accusations of discrimination and legal challenges. Over 50 articles highlight the policy’s rollout, internal PP divisions, and backlash from left-wing parties and human rights groups.
hotspots.affect: The 'national priority' policy commands attention due to its moral charge, polarized contention, and perceived threat to Spain’s constitutional principles of equality and non-discrimination. Its adoption by mainstream conservatives signals a normalization of far-right rhetoric, amplifying affective investment from both supporters and critics.

hotspots.title: Trump administration’s medical marijuana reclassification
hotspots.blurb: The Trump administration announced the reclassification of state-licensed medical marijuana from Schedule I to Schedule III, easing federal restrictions on research and tax deductions for cannabis businesses. Over 40 articles cover the DOJ’s move, its legal and economic implications, and reactions from lawmakers, industry leaders, and advocacy groups. The shift stops short of full legalization but marks a historic policy change.
hotspots.affect: This reclassification commands attention due to its high-stakes policy implications, moral framing around drug policy reform, and polarized reactions from conservative and progressive camps. The move is seen as both a triumph for cannabis advocates and a political maneuver ahead of midterms, amplifying affective investment across the spectrum.

hotspots.title: Hungary’s Tisza Party landslide and reforms
hotspots.blurb: Hungary’s opposition Tisza Party, led by Péter Magyar, won a two-thirds parliamentary majority in April 2026, ending Viktor Orbán’s 16-year rule. Over 60 articles detail the new government’s plans to open communist-era spy archives, adopt the euro, and reverse Fidesz’s illiberal policies. The shift has drawn international attention, with EU leaders hopeful for improved relations and U.S. investors optimistic about economic stability.
hotspots.affect: The Tisza Party’s victory commands attention due to its historic nature, the moral framing of Hungary’s democratic backsliding under Orbán, and the high stakes of EU funding and geopolitical alignment. The affective investment is amplified by cross-party reactions, from triumphant opposition figures to panicked Fidesz loyalists.

hotspots.title: Pete Hegseth’s Pentagon purges and Iran tensions
hotspots.blurb: U.S. Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth has overseen a series of high-profile firings, including Navy Secretary John Phelan and Army Chief of Staff Randy George, amid escalating tensions with Iran. Over 35 articles detail the purges, Hegseth’s religious rhetoric, and the military’s shifting priorities under Trump. The firings coincide with a U.S. naval blockade of Iran and debates over military readiness and vaccine mandates.
hotspots.affect: Hegseth’s purges command attention due to their dramatic framing as a leadership crisis, the moral charge of religious rhetoric in military affairs, and the high stakes of U.S.-Iran tensions. The affective investment is amplified by cross-party outrage, with critics accusing Hegseth of politicizing the Pentagon and undermining national security.

hotspots.title: Reform UK’s ‘patriotic’ school agenda
hotspots.blurb: Reform UK has proposed a sweeping overhaul of England’s school curriculum, mandating the display of the Union flag and King’s portrait in every classroom, alongside a ‘patriotic’ history curriculum. Over 30 articles cover the party’s plans, including opposition from teachers’ unions, LGBTQ+ groups, and political rivals. The proposals come as Reform UK gains ground in local elections, with Nigel Farage positioning the party as a challenger to Labour.
hotspots.affect: Reform UK’s school agenda commands attention due to its moral charge around national identity, polarized contention with progressive groups, and the high stakes of cultural policy. The affective investment is amplified by cross-party reactions, from triumphant nationalist rhetoric to accusations of fascism and propaganda.
